# frozen_string_literal: true

module Spree
  class SimpleOrderContents
    attr_accessor :order

    def initialize(order)
      @order = order
    end

    # Add a line items to the order if there is inventory to do so
    # and populate Promotions
    #
    # @param [Spree::Variant] variant The variant the line_item should
    #   be associated with
    # @param [Integer] quantity The line_item quantity
    # @param [Hash] options Options for the adding proccess
    #   Valid options:
    #     shipment: [Spree::Shipment] LineItem target shipment
    #
    # @return [Spree::LineItem]
    def add(variant, quantity = 1, options = {})
      line_item = add_to_line_item(variant, quantity, options)
      after_add_or_remove(line_item, options)
    end

    def remove(variant, quantity = 1, options = {})
      line_item = remove_from_line_item(variant, quantity, options)
      after_add_or_remove(line_item, options)
    end

    def remove_line_item(line_item, options = {})
      order.line_items.destroy(line_item)
      after_add_or_remove(line_item, options)
    end

    def update_cart(params)
      if order.update(params)
        unless order.completed?
          order.line_items = order.line_items.select { |li| li.quantity > 0 }
          order.check_shipments_and_restart_checkout
        end
        reload_totals
        true
      else
        false
      end
    end

    def advance
      while @order.next; end
    end

    def approve(user: nil, name: nil)
      if user.blank? && name.blank?
        raise ArgumentError, "user or name must be specified"
      end

      order.update!(
        approver: user,
        approver_name: name,
        approved_at: Time.current
      )
    end

    private

    def after_add_or_remove(line_item, options = {})
      shipment = options[:shipment]
      shipment.present? ? shipment.update_amounts : order.check_shipments_and_restart_checkout
      reload_totals
      line_item
    end

    def reload_totals
      @order.recalculate
    end

    def add_to_line_item(variant, quantity, options = {})
      line_item = grab_line_item_by_variant(variant, false, options)

      line_item ||= order.line_items.new(
        quantity: 0,
        variant:,
        adjustments: []
      )

      permitted_attributes = Spree::PermittedAttributes.line_item_attributes.dup
      permitted_attributes << {admin_metadata: {}} if options[:admin_metadata].present?

      line_item.quantity += quantity.to_i
      line_item.options = ActionController::Parameters.new(options).permit(permitted_attributes).to_h

      line_item.target_shipment = options[:shipment]
      line_item.save!
      line_item
    end

    def remove_from_line_item(variant, quantity, options = {})
      line_item = grab_line_item_by_variant(variant, true, options)
      line_item.quantity -= quantity
      line_item.target_shipment = options[:shipment]

      if line_item.quantity <= 0
        order.line_items.destroy(line_item)
      else
        line_item.save!
      end

      line_item
    end

    def grab_line_item_by_variant(variant, raise_error = false, options = {})
      line_item = order.find_line_item_by_variant(variant, options)

      if line_item.blank? && raise_error
        raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound, "Line item not found for variant #{variant.sku}"
      end

      line_item
    end
  end
end

4	class Spree::OrderCancellations
5	  extend ActiveModel::Translation
6	
7	  # If you need to message a third party service when an item is canceled then

9	  #     #call(unit_cancels)
10	  class_attribute :short_ship_tax_notifier
11	
12	  class << self
13	    def send_cancellation_mailer=(value)
14	      @send_cancellation_mailer = value
15	
16	      unless value
17	        Spree.deprecator.warn "Using the `:send_cancellation_mailer` class " \
18	          "attribute is deprecated in favor of including or omitting the " \
19	          "`Spree::OrderInventoryCancellationMailerSubscriber` from " \
20	          "`Spree::Config.environment.subscribers` in an initializer."
21	      end
22	    end

25	      @send_cancellation_mailer || @send_cancellation_mailer.nil?
26	    end
27	  end

29	  def initialize(order)
30	    @order = order
31	  end
32	
33	  # Marks inventory units short shipped. Adjusts the order based on the value of the inventory.
34	  # Sends an email to the customer about what inventory has been short shipped.
35	  #
36	  # @api public
37	  #
38	  # @param [Array<InventoryUnit>] inventory_units the inventory units to be short shipped
39	  # @param [Spree.user_class] created_by the system or person that is short shipping the inventory unit
40	  #
41	  # @return [Array<UnitCancel>] the units that have been canceled due to short shipping
42	  def short_ship(inventory_units, created_by: nil)
43	    if inventory_units.map(&:order_id).uniq != [@order.id]
44	      raise ArgumentError, "Not all inventory units belong to this order"
45	    end
46	
47	    unit_cancels = []
48	
49	    Spree::OrderMutex.with_lock!(@order) do
50	      Spree::InventoryUnit.transaction do
51	        inventory_units.each do |iu|
52	          unit_cancels << short_ship_unit(iu, created_by:)
53	        end
54	      end
55	
56	      @order.recalculate
57	
58	      short_ship_tax_notifier&.call(unit_cancels)
59	    end
60	
61	    Spree::Bus.publish(:order_short_shipped, order: @order, inventory_units:)
62	    unit_cancels
63	  end
64	
65	  # Marks inventory unit canceled. Optionally allows specifying the reason why and who is performing the action.
66	  #
67	  # @api public
68	  #
69	  # @param [InventoryUnit] inventory_unit the inventory unit to be canceled
70	  # @param [String] reason the reason that you are canceling the inventory unit
71	  # @param [Spree.user_class] created_by the system or person that is canceling the inventory unit
72	  #
73	  # @return [UnitCancel] the unit that has been canceled
74	  def cancel_unit(inventory_unit, reason: Spree::UnitCancel::DEFAULT_REASON, created_by: nil)
75	    unit_cancel = nil
76	
77	    Spree::OrderMutex.with_lock!(@order) do
78	      unit_cancel = Spree::UnitCancel.create!(
79	        inventory_unit:,
80	        reason:,
81	        created_by:
82	      )
83	
84	      inventory_unit.cancel!
85	    end
86	
87	    unit_cancel
88	  end
89	
90	  # Reimburses inventory units due to cancellation.
91	  #
92	  # @api public
93	  # @param [Array<InventoryUnit>] inventory_units the inventory units to be reimbursed
94	  # @param [Spree.user_class] created_by the user that is performing this action
95	  # @return [Reimbursement] the reimbursement for inventory being canceled
96	  def reimburse_units(inventory_units, created_by:)
97	    reimbursement = nil
98	
99	    Spree::OrderMutex.with_lock!(@order) do
100	      return_items = inventory_units.map(&:current_or_new_return_item)
101	      reimbursement = Spree::Reimbursement.new(order: @order, return_items:)
102	      reimbursement.return_all(created_by:)
103	    end
104	
105	    reimbursement
106	  end

111	    unit_cancel = Spree::UnitCancel.create!(
112	      inventory_unit:,
113	      reason: Spree::UnitCancel::SHORT_SHIP,
114	      created_by:
115	    )
116	    unit_cancel.adjust!
117	    inventory_unit.cancel!

# frozen_string_literal: true

# This class represents all of the actions one can take to modify an Order after it is complete
class Spree::OrderCancellations
  extend ActiveModel::Translation

  # If you need to message a third party service when an item is canceled then
  # set short_ship_tax_notifier to an object that responds to:
  #     #call(unit_cancels)
  class_attribute :short_ship_tax_notifier

  class << self
    def send_cancellation_mailer=(value)
      @send_cancellation_mailer = value

      unless value
        Spree.deprecator.warn "Using the `:send_cancellation_mailer` class " \
          "attribute is deprecated in favor of including or omitting the " \
          "`Spree::OrderInventoryCancellationMailerSubscriber` from " \
          "`Spree::Config.environment.subscribers` in an initializer."
      end
    end

    def send_cancellation_mailer
      @send_cancellation_mailer || @send_cancellation_mailer.nil?
    end
  end

  def initialize(order)
    @order = order
  end

  # Marks inventory units short shipped. Adjusts the order based on the value of the inventory.
  # Sends an email to the customer about what inventory has been short shipped.
  #
  # @api public
  #
  # @param [Array<InventoryUnit>] inventory_units the inventory units to be short shipped
  # @param [Spree.user_class] created_by the system or person that is short shipping the inventory unit
  #
  # @return [Array<UnitCancel>] the units that have been canceled due to short shipping
  def short_ship(inventory_units, created_by: nil)
    if inventory_units.map(&:order_id).uniq != [@order.id]
      raise ArgumentError, "Not all inventory units belong to this order"
    end

    unit_cancels = []

    Spree::OrderMutex.with_lock!(@order) do
      Spree::InventoryUnit.transaction do
        inventory_units.each do |iu|
          unit_cancels << short_ship_unit(iu, created_by:)
        end
      end

      @order.recalculate

      short_ship_tax_notifier&.call(unit_cancels)
    end

    Spree::Bus.publish(:order_short_shipped, order: @order, inventory_units:)
    unit_cancels
  end

  # Marks inventory unit canceled. Optionally allows specifying the reason why and who is performing the action.
  #
  # @api public
  #
  # @param [InventoryUnit] inventory_unit the inventory unit to be canceled
  # @param [String] reason the reason that you are canceling the inventory unit
  # @param [Spree.user_class] created_by the system or person that is canceling the inventory unit
  #
  # @return [UnitCancel] the unit that has been canceled
  def cancel_unit(inventory_unit, reason: Spree::UnitCancel::DEFAULT_REASON, created_by: nil)
    unit_cancel = nil

    Spree::OrderMutex.with_lock!(@order) do
      unit_cancel = Spree::UnitCancel.create!(
        inventory_unit:,
        reason:,
        created_by:
      )

      inventory_unit.cancel!
    end

    unit_cancel
  end

  # Reimburses inventory units due to cancellation.
  #
  # @api public
  # @param [Array<InventoryUnit>] inventory_units the inventory units to be reimbursed
  # @param [Spree.user_class] created_by the user that is performing this action
  # @return [Reimbursement] the reimbursement for inventory being canceled
  def reimburse_units(inventory_units, created_by:)
    reimbursement = nil

    Spree::OrderMutex.with_lock!(@order) do
      return_items = inventory_units.map(&:current_or_new_return_item)
      reimbursement = Spree::Reimbursement.new(order: @order, return_items:)
      reimbursement.return_all(created_by:)
    end

    reimbursement
  end

  private

  def short_ship_unit(inventory_unit, created_by: nil)
    unit_cancel = Spree::UnitCancel.create!(
      inventory_unit:,
      reason: Spree::UnitCancel::SHORT_SHIP,
      created_by:
    )
    unit_cancel.adjust!
    inventory_unit.cancel!

    unit_cancel
  end
end

file.content:
# frozen_string_literal: true

module Spree
  class OrderMutex < Spree::Base
    class LockFailed < StandardError; end

    belongs_to :order, class_name: "Spree::Order", optional: true

    scope :expired, -> { where(arel_table[:created_at].lteq(Spree::Config[:order_mutex_max_age].seconds.ago)) }

    class << self
      # Obtain a lock on an order, execute the supplied block and then release the lock.
      # Raise a LockFailed exception immediately if we cannot obtain the lock.
      # We raise instead of blocking to avoid tying up multiple server processes waiting for the lock.
      def with_lock!(order)
        raise ArgumentError, "order must be supplied" if order.nil?

        # limit the maximum lock time just in case a lock is somehow left in place accidentally
        expired.where(order:).delete_all

        begin
          order_mutex = create!(order:)
        rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique
          error = LockFailed.new("Could not obtain lock on order #{order.id}")
          logger.error error.inspect
          raise error
        end

        yield
      ensure
        order_mutex&.destroy
      end
    end
  end
end

  class OrderUpdateAttributes
    # @param order [Order] existing (persisted) order
    # @param attributes [Hash] attributes which are assigned to the new order.

    def initialize(order, attributes, request_env: nil)
      @order = order
      @attributes = attributes.dup
      @payments_attributes = @attributes.delete(:payments_attributes) || []
      @request_env = request_env
    end

      order.validate_payments_attributes(@payments_attributes)

      assign_order_attributes
      assign_payments_attributes

      order.save
    end

    alias_method :apply, :call

    attr_reader :attributes, :payments_attributes, :order

    end

      end

I now have a complete picture. Here's the full audit.

---

# Order Contract Audit — Solidus

## Step 1 — Orientation

**What it is:** Solidus is a full-stack e-commerce framework for Ruby on Rails (the maintained fork of Spree), distributed as a set of cooperating gems under one repo. ~2,677 files, Ruby + ERB + JS.

**How it's organized (multi-gem):**
- `core/` — domain models, services, state machines, config (`Spree::*`). The heart. (`core/app/models/spree`, `core/lib/spree`)
- `api/` — JSON REST API controllers (`Spree::Api::*`)
- `backend/` — legacy admin UI (controllers/views/helpers, `Spree::Admin::*`)
- `admin/` — new admin UI built on ViewComponent (`SolidusAdmin::*`)
- `promotions/` — modern promotion system (`SolidusPromotions::*`)
- `legacy_promotions/` — old promotion system (`Spree::Promotion::*`, plus `Spree::OrderContents`)
- `lib/`, `sample/`, `tasks/` — meta-gem, seed data, rake tasks

**The central model:** `Spree::Order` at `core/app/models/spree/order.rb:21` ("the heart of the Solidus system" — cart while shopping, permanent record once complete). Everything hangs off it: `line_items`, `payments`, `shipments`, `adjustments`/`all_adjustments`, `return_authorizations`, `inventory_units` (through shipments), promotions. It's a `Spree::Base` (`core/app/models/spree/base.rb:3`) subclass and carries a checkout **state machine** (`core/app/models/spree/core/state_machines/order.rb` + `.../order/class_methods.rb`). Recalculation is delegated to `Spree::OrderUpdater` (`core/app/models/spree/order_updater.rb:4`, configurable via `Spree::Config.order_recalculator_class`).

Map of files the next steps touch:
- Model: `core/app/models/spree/order.rb`, concern `core/app/models/spree/order/payments.rb`, `core/app/models/spree/order/number_generator.rb`
- Order-attached services: `order_updater.rb`, `in_memory_order_updater.rb`, `simple_order_contents.rb`, `order_shipping.rb`, `order_cancellations.rb`, `order_inventory.rb`, `order_merger.rb`, `order_taxation.rb`, `order_mutex.rb`, `order_update_attributes.rb`, `fulfilment_changer.rb`
- Config: `core/lib/spree/app_configuration.rb` (the `order_*_class` swap points)

## Step 2 — The Order contract and what it's built on

**Superclass / mixins (the shared pieces):**
- `Spree::Base < ActiveRecord::Base` — `core/app/models/spree/base.rb:3`; pulls in `Spree::Core::Permalinks` and `Spree::RansackableAttributes` (`base.rb:4-5`).
- State machine: `include ::Spree::Config.state_machines.order` — `order.rb:26`, resolving to `Spree::Core::StateMachines::Order` at `core/app/models/spree/core/state_machines/order.rb:6` (instance: `checkout_steps`, `has_checkout_step?`, `passed_checkout_step?`, `can_go_to_state?`) + `core/app/models/spree/core/state_machines/order/class_methods.rb:7` (the `checkout_flow`/`go_to_state`/`state_machine` DSL).
- `include Spree::Order::Payments` — `order.rb:28` → `core/app/models/spree/order/payments.rb:5` (`process_payments!`, `authorize_payments!`, `capture_payments!`, `unprocessed_payments`).
- `include Metadata` — `order.rb:29` → `core/app/models/concerns/spree/metadata.rb:4` (adds `customer_metadata`/`admin_metadata` JSON columns + validation).
- `extend Spree::DisplayMoney` + `money_methods(...)` — `order.rb:42-59` → `core/app/models/concerns/spree/display_money.rb` (the `display_*` money accessors).

**Associations other records hang off it (the teardown surface — note the `dependent: :destroy`):**
- `line_items` (`:destroy`) `order.rb:93`; `variants`/`products` through them `94-95`
- `shipments` (`:destroy`) `order.rb:98`; `inventory_units` through shipments `103`; `cartons` `104`
- `adjustments` (polymorphic adjustable, `:destroy`, autosave) `order.rb:107`; `line_item_adjustments` `108`; `shipment_adjustments` `109`; `all_adjustments` (`:destroy`) `110-114`
- `payments` (`:destroy`) `order.rb:117`; `valid_store_credit_payments` `118`; `refunds` through payments `125`
- `return_authorizations` (`:destroy`) `order.rb:121`; `return_items`/`customer_returns` `122-123`; `reimbursements` `124`
- `state_changes` (polymorphic stateful) `order.rb:128`
- `belongs_to`: `user`/`created_by`/`approver`/`canceler` (UserClassHandle), `bill_address`/`ship_address` (aliased billing/shipping), `store` — `order.rb:78-131`
- `accepts_nested_attributes_for :line_items, :bill_address, :ship_address, :payments, :shipments` — `order.rb:133-137`

**Contract a teammate must know before changing it:**
- Recalculation is delegated: `delegate :recalculate, to: :recalculator` (`order.rb:165`), `recalculator` memoizes `Spree::Config.order_recalculator_class.new(self)` (`order.rb:281`). `updater` is a deprecated alias (`order.rb:284`).
- Swappable collaborators via config: `contents` (`order.rb:310`), `shipping` (`314`), `cancellations` (`318`), `merge!`→`order_merger_class` (`457`), number gen (`337`).
- Public mutation/teardown API: `empty!` (`order.rb:461` — destroys line_items/adjustments/shipments, publishes `:order_emptied`, recalculates), `fulfill!` (`421`), `finalize` (private, `758`), `after_cancel`/`cancel_shipments!`/`cancel_payments!` (`846-867`), `create_proposed_shipments`/`create_shipments_for_line_item`/`check_shipments_and_restart_checkout` (`504-531`), `restart_checkout_flow` (`536`).
- Events published on `Spree::Bus`: `:order_emptied` (`465`), `:order_finalized` (`774`), `:order_canceled` (`853`), `:order_recalculated` (from updater).
- Business-logic predicates the whole app relies on: `completed?` (`order.rb:251`), `paid?` (`428`), `payment_required?` (`264`), `checkout_allowed?` (`259`), `can_ship?` (`407`), `allow_cancel?` (`297`), `outstanding_balance` (`382`).

## Step 3 — How an order is built and recalculated

**Build (cart → order):**
1. Storefront/admin/API create an Order: `Spree::Core::ControllerHelpers::Order#current_order` (`core/app/helpers/spree/core/controller_helpers/order.rb:15`), or `Spree::Core::Importer::Order.import` (`core/lib/spree/core/importer/order.rb:7`), or `Spree::Api::OrdersController#create` (`api/app/controllers/spree/api/orders_controller.rb:31`). Creation runs callbacks `associate_store`/`set_currency`/`generate_order_number`/`create_token`/`link_by_email` (`order.rb:140-149`).
2. Items added through contents: `Spree::SimpleOrderContents#add`/`remove`/`remove_line_item`/`update_cart` (`core/app/models/spree/simple_order_contents.rb:22,27,32,37`). Each goes through `after_add_or_remove` (`simple_order_contents.rb:68`) → `shipment.update_amounts` or `order.check_shipments_and_restart_checkout` → `reload_totals` → **`order.recalculate`** (`simple_order_contents.rb:75-77`).
3. Checkout advances via the state machine `:next` transitions (`order.next`, `contents.advance` `simple_order_contents.rb:50`), wired in `class_methods.rb:38-118`.

**Recalculation fan-out — the order it runs** (`Spree::OrderUpdater#recalculate`, `core/app/models/spree/order_updater.rb:19`; identical structure in the persist-flag variant `Spree::InMemoryOrderUpdater#recalculate`, `core/app/models/spree/in_memory_order_updater.rb:26`):
1. `recalculate_line_item_prices` (`order_updater.rb:245`) — re-price line items if `recalculate_cart_prices`.
2. `recalculate_item_count` (`order_updater.rb:185`).
3. `update_shipment_amounts` → `shipment.update_amounts` for each shipment (`order_updater.rb:141`).
4. `update_totals` (`order_updater.rb:134`): `recalculate_payment_total` (`150`) → `recalculate_item_total` (`191`) → `recalculate_shipment_total` (`156`) → `update_adjustment_total` (`169`).
5. `update_adjustment_total` → **`update_adjustments`** (`order_updater.rb:114`): `update_promotions` (`Spree::Config.promotions.order_adjuster_class.new(order).call`, `order_updater.rb:218`) → `update_tax_adjustments` (`Spree::Config.tax_adjuster_class.new(order).adjust!`, `222`) → `recalculate_item_totals` (per-item `item_total_class.recalculate!`, `230`). Then sums adjustment/included-tax/additional-tax totals and `recalculate_order_total` (`163`).
6. If `order.completed?` (`order_updater.rb:25`): `recalculate_payment_state` (`66`) → `update_shipments` (each `shipment.update_state`, `146`) → `recalculate_shipment_state` (`47`).
7. `persist_totals` → `order.save!` (`order_updater.rb:198`), then publishes `:order_recalculated` (`order_updater.rb:32`).

Stock coordination feeds shipments during checkout: `create_proposed_shipments` → `Spree::Config.stock.coordinator_class.new(self).shipments` (`order.rb:504-513`), built by `Spree::Stock::SimpleCoordinator` (`core/app/models/spree/stock/simple_coordinator.rb`). State-machine `after_transition` also calls `order.recalculate` (`class_methods.rb:107-109`).

## Step 4 — Exhaustive dependent audit

Grouped by area; each is an individual dependent with file:line and how it uses the order. (Verified via `sense_graph`/`sense_blast` on the reopened `Spree::Order` class, spot-checked with grep.)

### Core domain models & order-attached services
- `Spree::OrderUpdater` `core/app/models/spree/order_updater.rb:4` — recalculates all totals/states (default `order_recalculator_class`).
- `Spree::InMemoryOrderUpdater` `core/app/models/spree/in_memory_order_updater.rb:6` — persist-flag recalculation variant.
- `Spree::SimpleOrderContents` `core/app/models/spree/simple_order_contents.rb:4` — add/remove/update line items, advance, approve; calls `order.recalculate`.
- `Spree::OrderContents` (legacy) `legacy_promotions/app/models/spree/order_contents.rb:7` — legacy `update_cart`, guards on `order.completed?`.
- `Spree::OrderShipping` `core/app/models/spree/order_shipping.rb:45` — `ship` creates cartons, transitions inventory units.
- `Spree::OrderCancellations` `core/app/models/spree/order_cancellations.rb:4` — `short_ship`/`cancel_unit`/`reimburse_units` (post-complete teardown), recalculates under mutex.
- `Spree::OrderInventory` `core/app/models/spree/order_inventory.rb:21,85,120` — adds/removes inventory units per line item; branches on `order.completed?`.
- `Spree::OrderMerger` `core/app/models/spree/order_merger.rb` — merges a guest cart into another order (`merge!`, `order.rb:457`).

- `Spree::MergeableOrdersFinder#call` `core/app/models/spree/mergeable_orders_finder.rb:38` — finds incomplete orders to merge.

## Step 5 — How order state/completeness is guarded

The codebase pivots almost entirely on `Order#completed?` (`order.rb:251`, `completed_at.present?`) and the state-machine `state`/`complete?`. Guard sites:

**Recalculation gates the post-complete branch** (payment/shipment-state recompute only when complete): `order_updater.rb:25` and `in_memory_order_updater.rb:50` (`if order.completed?`).

**Mutation guards in models/services:**
- `Spree::LineItem#recalculate_price` `core/app/models/spree/line_item.rb:136` — `return if order.completed?` (don't reprice completed orders).
- `Spree::Payment#update_order` `core/app/models/spree/payment.rb:201` — only recalculates `if order.completed? || completed? || void?`.
- `Spree::PaymentMethod::CreditCard` `core/app/models/spree/payment_method/credit_card.rb:28` — branch on `order.completed?`.
- `Spree::OrderInventory` `core/app/models/spree/order_inventory.rb:21,85,120` — finalize/restock only `if order.completed?`.
- `Spree::SimpleOrderContents#update_cart` `core/app/models/spree/simple_order_contents.rb:39` and legacy `Spree::OrderContents` `legacy_promotions/app/models/spree/order_contents.rb:9` — `unless order.completed?` before trimming line items / restarting checkout.
- `Spree::FulfilmentChanger` `core/app/models/spree/fulfilment_changer.rb:149` — stock-location move only when `order.completed?`.
- `Spree::Order#create_proposed_shipments` `order.rb:505` — raises `CannotRebuildShipments` if `completed?`; `check_shipments_and_restart_checkout` `order.rb:526` — only acts `if !completed? && shipments.all?(&:pending?)`.
- `Spree::Shipment#ensure_can_destroy` `core/app/models/spree/shipment.rb:366` (before_destroy) — aborts if `shipped? || canceled?`.

**Scope-based guards (set-level):**
- `Spree::Order.complete` / `.incomplete` / `.canceled` / `.not_canceled` `order.rb:205-219`.
- `Spree::AdjustmentSource#remove_adjustments_from_incomplete_orders` `core/app/models/concerns/spree/adjustment_source.rb:5` — `.merge(Spree::Order.incomplete)` (used by tax rates & legacy promo actions).
- `SolidusPromotions::Benefit#raise_for_adjustments_for_completed_orders` `promotions/app/models/solidus_promotions/benefit.rb:341` — before_destroy aborts if any `Spree::Order.complete` adjustment exists.

- `Spree::Api::OrdersController#update` `api/app/controllers/spree/api/orders_controller.rb:50` — `if @order.complete?` branch.

**State-machine guards** (`class_methods.rb`): `allow_cancel?` gates `cancel` (`order.rb:297`, requires `completed? && state != canceled`); `all_inventory_units_returned?` gates `return` (`order.rb:302`); `can_go_to_state?`/`passed_checkout_step?` gate forward transitions (`state_machines/order.rb:27-39`); before_complete validation chain `class_methods.rb:95-101`.

**Concurrency guard:** `Spree::OrderMutex.with_lock!(order)` `core/app/models/spree/order_mutex.rb:15` — DB-row advisory lock wrapping cancellations/reimbursements (`order_cancellations.rb:49,77,99`).

## Step 6 — Blast radius (ranked by risk)

`sense_blast` rates `Spree::Order` **high risk**: 135 direct callers, 272 affected symbols, 126 affected files, 36 affected test files; 153 production vs 57 test affected. Because the change touches storage, recalculation, and **teardown** (`dependent: :destroy`, `empty!`, cancel/short-ship), the highest risk sits in the recalculation/teardown path and the records that cascade-destroy with the order.

**Tier 1 — almost certainly breaks; must change in lockstep**
1. `Spree::OrderUpdater` / `Spree::InMemoryOrderUpdater` `order_updater.rb:4`, `in_memory_order_updater.rb:6` — the recalculation contract itself; any change to how totals/states store flows through here.
2. The `dependent: :destroy` associations & their `before_destroy` guards — `line_items`/`shipments`/`payments`/`adjustments`/`all_adjustments`/`return_authorizations` (`order.rb:93-121`); teardown order and the abort guards (`shipment.rb:366`, `benefit.rb:341`) are load-bearing.
3. `Spree::SimpleOrderContents` (+ legacy `OrderContents`) `simple_order_contents.rb:4` — every add/remove/update_cart calls `recalculate`.
4. `Spree::Order#empty!` / `finalize` / `after_cancel` / `create_proposed_shipments` `order.rb:461,758,846,504` — the explicit teardown/rebuild API.
5. State machine `class_methods.rb` + `order.rb:26` — `after_transition … order.recalculate` (`:107`), `finalize`, `after_cancel`/`after_resume` hooks.

**Tier 2 — high risk; derive state/totals from the order**
6. `Spree::Payment` + `Payment::Processing#capture!` `payment.rb:8,201`, `processing.rb:73` — `update_order` recalculation on completion.
7. `Spree::Shipment` `shipment.rb:6` — `update_amounts`/`update_state`/`recalculate_state`/`ensure_can_destroy`.
8. `Spree::OrderInventory` / `Spree::OrderShipping` / `Spree::OrderCancellations` / `Spree::FulfilmentChanger` — inventory mutation + recalculation, all `completed?`-sensitive.
9. `Spree::Stock::SimpleCoordinator` & package/builder/finalizer `simple_coordinator.rb:84`, `package.rb`, `inventory_unit_builder.rb`, `inventory_units_finalizer.rb` — shipment construction.
10. Tax: `Spree::OrderTaxation` `order_taxation.rb:51`, `Spree::TaxRate` `tax_rate.rb:4` — adjustments + `remove_adjustments_from_incomplete_orders`.
11. Promotions adjusters (both gems): `SolidusPromotions::Benefit`/conditions and `Spree::Promotion`/rules/actions — invoked from `update_promotions` and via adjustment cascade; the `Order.complete`/`incomplete` scope guards (`benefit.rb:341`, `adjustment_source.rb:5`, `promotion.rb:175`) must keep working.
12. `Spree::OrderMerger` / `MergeableOrdersFinder` `order_merger.rb`, `mergeable_orders_finder.rb:38` — merges destructively combine carts.
13. `Spree::Exchange` / `Spree::ReturnItem` / `Spree::ReturnAuthorization` / `Spree::ReimbursementType::Exchange` — returns rebuild shipments and recalc.

**Tier 3 — medium; read/orchestrate, fewer total-mutation assumptions**
14. API controllers (`Orders`, `Checkouts`, `LineItems`, `Payments`, `Shipments`, `CustomerReturns`, `ReturnAuthorizations`, `CouponCodes`, `Addresses`, `BaseController`) — drive create/checkout/teardown via the contract; `OrdersController#update` (`:50`) and `prevent_customer_metadata_update` (`:122`) are most exposed.
15. `Spree::Core::Importer::Order.import` `core/lib/spree/core/importer/order.rb:7` and `ControllerHelpers::Order` `controller_helpers/order.rb:15` — order construction paths.

**Tier 4 — low; read-only/display/auth**
16. Admin (legacy + new) controllers/components/views, `BackendConfiguration#menu_items`, `PermissionSets::Order*`, thumbnail/index components, promotion rule `applicable?` predicates — display, listing, authorization; break loudest if an association name or `completed?`/`total` semantics change.

**Must be re-verified after the change:** total/state correctness through `recalculate` (item/shipment/adjustment/tax/payment), the complete-vs-incomplete branch, cascade-destroy ordering + the `before_destroy` aborts, `empty!`/cancel/short-ship/exchange flows, promotion & tax adjustment recompute in both promo gems, `Spree::Bus` events (`:order_recalculated`, `:order_emptied`, `:order_finalized`, `:order_canceled`), and `OrderMutex` concurrency.

I now have a complete picture. Here's the full audit.

---

# Order Contract Audit — Solidus

## Step 1 — Orientation

**What it is:** Solidus is a full-stack e-commerce framework for Ruby on Rails (the maintained fork of Spree), distributed as a set of cooperating gems under one repo. ~2,677 files, Ruby + ERB + JS.

**How it's organized (multi-gem):**
- `core/` — domain models, services, state machines, config (`Spree::*`). The heart. (`core/app/models/spree`, `core/lib/spree`)
- `api/` — JSON REST API controllers (`Spree::Api::*`)
- `backend/` — legacy admin UI (controllers/views/helpers, `Spree::Admin::*`)
- `admin/` — new admin UI built on ViewComponent (`SolidusAdmin::*`)
- `promotions/` — modern promotion system (`SolidusPromotions::*`)
- `legacy_promotions/` — old promotion system (`Spree::Promotion::*`, plus `Spree::OrderContents`)
- `lib/`, `sample/`, `tasks/` — meta-gem, seed data, rake tasks

**The central model:** `Spree::Order` at `core/app/models/spree/order.rb:21` ("the heart of the Solidus system" — cart while shopping, permanent record once complete). Everything hangs off it: `line_items`, `payments`, `shipments`, `adjustments`/`all_adjustments`, `return_authorizations`, `inventory_units` (through shipments), promotions. It's a `Spree::Base` (`core/app/models/spree/base.rb:3`) subclass and carries a checkout **state machine** (`core/app/models/spree/core/state_machines/order.rb` + `.../order/class_methods.rb`). Recalculation is delegated to `Spree::OrderUpdater` (`core/app/models/spree/order_updater.rb:4`, configurable via `Spree::Config.order_recalculator_class`).

Map of files the next steps touch:
- Model: `core/app/models/spree/order.rb`, concern `core/app/models/spree/order/payments.rb`, `core/app/models/spree/order/number_generator.rb`
- Order-attached services: `order_updater.rb`, `in_memory_order_updater.rb`, `simple_order_contents.rb`, `order_shipping.rb`, `order_cancellations.rb`, `order_inventory.rb`, `order_merger.rb`, `order_taxation.rb`, `order_mutex.rb`, `order_update_attributes.rb`, `fulfilment_changer.rb`
- Config: `core/lib/spree/app_configuration.rb` (the `order_*_class` swap points)

## Step 2 — The Order contract and what it's built on

**Superclass / mixins (the shared pieces):**
- `Spree::Base < ActiveRecord::Base` — `core/app/models/spree/base.rb:3`; pulls in `Spree::Core::Permalinks` and `Spree::RansackableAttributes` (`base.rb:4-5`).
- State machine: `include ::Spree::Config.state_machines.order` — `order.rb:26`, resolving to `Spree::Core::StateMachines::Order` at `core/app/models/spree/core/state_machines/order.rb:6` (instance: `checkout_steps`, `has_checkout_step?`, `passed_checkout_step?`, `can_go_to_state?`) + `core/app/models/spree/core/state_machines/order/class_methods.rb:7` (the `checkout_flow`/`go_to_state`/`state_machine` DSL).
- `include Spree::Order::Payments` — `order.rb:28` → `core/app/models/spree/order/payments.rb:5` (`process_payments!`, `authorize_payments!`, `capture_payments!`, `unprocessed_payments`).
- `include Metadata` — `order.rb:29` → `core/app/models/concerns/spree/metadata.rb:4` (adds `customer_metadata`/`admin_metadata` JSON columns + validation).
- `extend Spree::DisplayMoney` + `money_methods(...)` — `order.rb:42-59` → `core/app/models/concerns/spree/display_money.rb` (the `display_*` money accessors).

**Associations other records hang off it (the teardown surface — note the `dependent: :destroy`):**
- `line_items` (`:destroy`) `order.rb:93`; `variants`/`products` through them `94-95`
- `shipments` (`:destroy`) `order.rb:98`; `inventory_units` through shipments `103`; `cartons` `104`
- `adjustments` (polymorphic adjustable, `:destroy`, autosave) `order.rb:107`; `line_item_adjustments` `108`; `shipment_adjustments` `109`; `all_adjustments` (`:destroy`) `110-114`
- `payments` (`:destroy`) `order.rb:117`; `valid_store_credit_payments` `118`; `refunds` through payments `125`
- `return_authorizations` (`:destroy`) `order.rb:121`; `return_items`/`customer_returns` `122-123`; `reimbursements` `124`
- `state_changes` (polymorphic stateful) `order.rb:128`
- `belongs_to`: `user`/`created_by`/`approver`/`canceler` (UserClassHandle), `bill_address`/`ship_address` (aliased billing/shipping), `store` — `order.rb:78-131`
- `accepts_nested_attributes_for :line_items, :bill_address, :ship_address, :payments, :shipments` — `order.rb:133-137`

**Contract a teammate must know before changing it:**
- Recalculation is delegated: `delegate :recalculate, to: :recalculator` (`order.rb:165`), `recalculator` memoizes `Spree::Config.order_recalculator_class.new(self)` (`order.rb:281`). `updater` is a deprecated alias (`order.rb:284`).
- Swappable collaborators via config: `contents` (`order.rb:310`), `shipping` (`314`), `cancellations` (`318`), `merge!`→`order_merger_class` (`457`), number gen (`337`).
- Public mutation/teardown API: `empty!` (`order.rb:461` — destroys line_items/adjustments/shipments, publishes `:order_emptied`, recalculates), `fulfill!` (`421`), `finalize` (private, `758`), `after_cancel`/`cancel_shipments!`/`cancel_payments!` (`846-867`), `create_proposed_shipments`/`create_shipments_for_line_item`/`check_shipments_and_restart_checkout` (`504-531`), `restart_checkout_flow` (`536`).
- Events published on `Spree::Bus`: `:order_emptied` (`465`), `:order_finalized` (`774`), `:order_canceled` (`853`), `:order_recalculated` (from updater).
- Business-logic predicates the whole app relies on: `completed?` (`order.rb:251`), `paid?` (`428`), `payment_required?` (`264`), `checkout_allowed?` (`259`), `can_ship?` (`407`), `allow_cancel?` (`297`), `outstanding_balance` (`382`).

## Step 3 — How an order is built and recalculated

**Build (cart → order):**
1. Storefront/admin/API create an Order: `Spree::Core::ControllerHelpers::Order#current_order` (`core/app/helpers/spree/core/controller_helpers/order.rb:15`), or `Spree::Core::Importer::Order.import` (`core/lib/spree/core/importer/order.rb:7`), or `Spree::Api::OrdersController#create` (`api/app/controllers/spree/api/orders_controller.rb:31`). Creation runs callbacks `associate_store`/`set_currency`/`generate_order_number`/`create_token`/`link_by_email` (`order.rb:140-149`).
2. Items added through contents: `Spree::SimpleOrderContents#add`/`remove`/`remove_line_item`/`update_cart` (`core/app/models/spree/simple_order_contents.rb:22,27,32,37`). Each goes through `after_add_or_remove` (`simple_order_contents.rb:68`) → `shipment.update_amounts` or `order.check_shipments_and_restart_checkout` → `reload_totals` → **`order.recalculate`** (`simple_order_contents.rb:75-77`).
3. Checkout advances via the state machine `:next` transitions (`order.next`, `contents.advance` `simple_order_contents.rb:50`), wired in `class_methods.rb:38-118`.

**Recalculation fan-out — the order it runs** (`Spree::OrderUpdater#recalculate`, `core/app/models/spree/order_updater.rb:19`; identical structure in the persist-flag variant `Spree::InMemoryOrderUpdater#recalculate`, `core/app/models/spree/in_memory_order_updater.rb:26`):
1. `recalculate_line_item_prices` (`order_updater.rb:245`) — re-price line items if `recalculate_cart_prices`.
2. `recalculate_item_count` (`order_updater.rb:185`).
3. `update_shipment_amounts` → `shipment.update_amounts` for each shipment (`order_updater.rb:141`).
4. `update_totals` (`order_updater.rb:134`): `recalculate_payment_total` (`150`) → `recalculate_item_total` (`191`) → `recalculate_shipment_total` (`156`) → `update_adjustment_total` (`169`).
5. `update_adjustment_total` → **`update_adjustments`** (`order_updater.rb:114`): `update_promotions` (`Spree::Config.promotions.order_adjuster_class.new(order).call`, `order_updater.rb:218`) → `update_tax_adjustments` (`Spree::Config.tax_adjuster_class.new(order).adjust!`, `222`) → `recalculate_item_totals` (per-item `item_total_class.recalculate!`, `230`). Then sums adjustment/included-tax/additional-tax totals and `recalculate_order_total` (`163`).
6. If `order.completed?` (`order_updater.rb:25`): `recalculate_payment_state` (`66`) → `update_shipments` (each `shipment.update_state`, `146`) → `recalculate_shipment_state` (`47`).
7. `persist_totals` → `order.save!` (`order_updater.rb:198`), then publishes `:order_recalculated` (`order_updater.rb:32`).

Stock coordination feeds shipments during checkout: `create_proposed_shipments` → `Spree::Config.stock.coordinator_class.new(self).shipments` (`order.rb:504-513`), built by `Spree::Stock::SimpleCoordinator` (`core/app/models/spree/stock/simple_coordinator.rb`). State-machine `after_transition` also calls `order.recalculate` (`class_methods.rb:107-109`).

## Step 5 — How order state/completeness is guarded

The codebase pivots almost entirely on `Order#completed?` (`order.rb:251`, `completed_at.present?`) and the state-machine `state`/`complete?`. Guard sites:

**Recalculation gates the post-complete branch** (payment/shipment-state recompute only when complete): `order_updater.rb:25` and `in_memory_order_updater.rb:50` (`if order.completed?`).

**Mutation guards in models/services:**
- `Spree::LineItem#recalculate_price` `core/app/models/spree/line_item.rb:136` — `return if order.completed?` (don't reprice completed orders).
- `Spree::Payment#update_order` `core/app/models/spree/payment.rb:201` — only recalculates `if order.completed? || completed? || void?`.
- `Spree::PaymentMethod::CreditCard` `core/app/models/spree/payment_method/credit_card.rb:28` — branch on `order.completed?`.
- `Spree::OrderInventory` `core/app/models/spree/order_inventory.rb:21,85,120` — finalize/restock only `if order.completed?`.
- `Spree::SimpleOrderContents#update_cart` `core/app/models/spree/simple_order_contents.rb:39` and legacy `Spree::OrderContents` `legacy_promotions/app/models/spree/order_contents.rb:9` — `unless order.completed?` before trimming line items / restarting checkout.
- `Spree::FulfilmentChanger` `core/app/models/spree/fulfilment_changer.rb:149` — stock-location move only when `order.completed?`.
- `Spree::Order#create_proposed_shipments` `order.rb:505` — raises `CannotRebuildShipments` if `completed?`; `check_shipments_and_restart_checkout` `order.rb:526` — only acts `if !completed? && shipments.all?(&:pending?)`.
- `Spree::Shipment#ensure_can_destroy` `core/app/models/spree/shipment.rb:366` (before_destroy) — aborts if `shipped? || canceled?`.

**Scope-based guards (set-level):**
- `Spree::Order.complete` / `.incomplete` / `.canceled` / `.not_canceled` `order.rb:205-219`.
- `Spree::AdjustmentSource#remove_adjustments_from_incomplete_orders` `core/app/models/concerns/spree/adjustment_source.rb:5` — `.merge(Spree::Order.incomplete)` (used by tax rates & legacy promo actions).
- `SolidusPromotions::Benefit#raise_for_adjustments_for_completed_orders` `promotions/app/models/solidus_promotions/benefit.rb:341` — before_destroy aborts if any `Spree::Order.complete` adjustment exists.
- `SolidusPromotions::Promotion#delete_cart_connections` `promotions/app/models/solidus_promotions/promotion.rb:175` — `Spree::Order.incomplete` only; `:72` skips complete orders unless `recalculate_complete_orders`.
- `Spree::Api::OrdersController#update` `api/app/controllers/spree/api/orders_controller.rb:50` — `if @order.complete?` branch.

**State-machine guards** (`class_methods.rb`): `allow_cancel?` gates `cancel` (`order.rb:297`, requires `completed? && state != canceled`); `all_inventory_units_returned?` gates `return` (`order.rb:302`); `can_go_to_state?`/`passed_checkout_step?` gate forward transitions (`state_machines/order.rb:27-39`); before_complete validation chain `class_methods.rb:95-101`.

**Concurrency guard:** `Spree::OrderMutex.with_lock!(order)` `core/app/models/spree/order_mutex.rb:15` — DB-row advisory lock wrapping cancellations/reimbursements (`order_cancellations.rb:49,77,99`).

## Step 6 — Blast radius (ranked by risk)

`sense_blast` rates `Spree::Order` **high risk**: 135 direct callers, 272 affected symbols, 126 affected files, 36 affected test files; 153 production vs 57 test affected. Because the change touches storage, recalculation, and **teardown** (`dependent: :destroy`, `empty!`, cancel/short-ship), the highest risk sits in the recalculation/teardown path and the records that cascade-destroy with the order.

**Tier 1 — almost certainly breaks; must change in lockstep**
1. `Spree::OrderUpdater` / `Spree::InMemoryOrderUpdater` `order_updater.rb:4`, `in_memory_order_updater.rb:6` — the recalculation contract itself; any change to how totals/states store flows through here.
2. The `dependent: :destroy` associations & their `before_destroy` guards — `line_items`/`shipments`/`payments`/`adjustments`/`all_adjustments`/`return_authorizations` (`order.rb:93-121`); teardown order and the abort guards (`shipment.rb:366`, `benefit.rb:341`) are load-bearing.
3. `Spree::SimpleOrderContents` (+ legacy `OrderContents`) `simple_order_contents.rb:4` — every add/remove/update_cart calls `recalculate`.
4. `Spree::Order#empty!` / `finalize` / `after_cancel` / `create_proposed_shipments` `order.rb:461,758,846,504` — the explicit teardown/rebuild API.
5. State machine `class_methods.rb` + `order.rb:26` — `after_transition … order.recalculate` (`:107`), `finalize`, `after_cancel`/`after_resume` hooks.

**Tier 2 — high risk; derive state/totals from the order**
6. `Spree::Payment` + `Payment::Processing#capture!` `payment.rb:8,201`, `processing.rb:73` — `update_order` recalculation on completion.
7. `Spree::Shipment` `shipment.rb:6` — `update_amounts`/`update_state`/`recalculate_state`/`ensure_can_destroy`.
8. `Spree::OrderInventory` / `Spree::OrderShipping` / `Spree::OrderCancellations` / `Spree::FulfilmentChanger` — inventory mutation + recalculation, all `completed?`-sensitive.
9. `Spree::Stock::SimpleCoordinator` & package/builder/finalizer `simple_coordinator.rb:84`, `package.rb`, `inventory_unit_builder.rb`, `inventory_units_finalizer.rb` — shipment construction.
10. Tax: `Spree::OrderTaxation` `order_taxation.rb:51`, `Spree::TaxRate` `tax_rate.rb:4` — adjustments + `remove_adjustments_from_incomplete_orders`.
11. Promotions adjusters (both gems): `SolidusPromotions::Benefit`/conditions and `Spree::Promotion`/rules/actions — invoked from `update_promotions` and via adjustment cascade; the `Order.complete`/`incomplete` scope guards (`benefit.rb:341`, `adjustment_source.rb:5`, `promotion.rb:175`) must keep working.
12. `Spree::OrderMerger` / `MergeableOrdersFinder` `order_merger.rb`, `mergeable_orders_finder.rb:38` — merges destructively combine carts.
13. `Spree::Exchange` / `Spree::ReturnItem` / `Spree::ReturnAuthorization` / `Spree::ReimbursementType::Exchange` — returns rebuild shipments and recalc.

**Tier 3 — medium; read/orchestrate, fewer total-mutation assumptions**
14. API controllers (`Orders`, `Checkouts`, `LineItems`, `Payments`, `Shipments`, `CustomerReturns`, `ReturnAuthorizations`, `CouponCodes`, `Addresses`, `BaseController`) — drive create/checkout/teardown via the contract; `OrdersController#update` (`:50`) and `prevent_customer_metadata_update` (`:122`) are most exposed.
15. `Spree::Core::Importer::Order.import` `core/lib/spree/core/importer/order.rb:7` and `ControllerHelpers::Order` `controller_helpers/order.rb:15` — order construction paths.

**Tier 4 — low; read-only/display/auth**
16. Admin (legacy + new) controllers/components/views, `BackendConfiguration#menu_items`, `PermissionSets::Order*`, thumbnail/index components, promotion rule `applicable?` predicates — display, listing, authorization; break loudest if an association name or `completed?`/`total` semantics change.

**Must be re-verified after the change:** total/state correctness through `recalculate` (item/shipment/adjustment/tax/payment), the complete-vs-incomplete branch, cascade-destroy ordering + the `before_destroy` aborts, `empty!`/cancel/short-ship/exchange flows, promotion & tax adjustment recompute in both promo gems, `Spree::Bus` events (`:order_recalculated`, `:order_emptied`, `:order_finalized`, `:order_canceled`), and `OrderMutex` concurrency.
